title: Turner Prize Shortlist: Art Exploring Identity and History
summary: The Turner Prize, one of the UK's most prestigious visual arts awards, has announced its four shortlisted artists for 2023. The nominees are Simeon Barclay, Kira Freije, Marguerite Humeau, and Tanoa Sasraku. Their works will be exhibited at the Middlesbrough Institute of Modern Art starting later this year, with the winner announced on December 10. Each shortlisted artist receives £10,000, and the winner gets £25,000. The prize was established in 1984 to stimulate public debate about contemporary British art and is named after painter J.M.W. Turner. This year's selection includes a spoken-word performance by Barclay about life in northern England, sculptures by Freije exploring human emotions, and works by Humeau and Sasraku addressing themes like the history of oil and mythical shapes. Jury chair Alex Farquharson praised the diversity and vitality of the works, noting they offer distinct perspectives on the world.

Three-dimensional art created by shaping materials.

background_read: The Turner Prize was founded in 1984 by the Tate gallery to promote contemporary British art. It is named after the landscape painter J.M.W. Turner (1775-1851). The prize is awarded annually to a British artist under 50 for an outstanding exhibition. Past winners include Damien Hirst, Steve McQueen, and Anish Kapoor. The prize often sparks debate about modern art.
Article_Structure: Main Points: The article announces the four shortlisted artists for the Turner Prize: Simeon Barclay, Kira Freije, Marguerite Humeau, and Tanoa Sasraku. Their works include a spoken-word performance, sculptures about emotions, and pieces examining oil history and mythical shapes. Purpose: To inform readers about the nominees and generate interest in the upcoming exhibition and award. Evidence Evaluation: The article relies on quotes from the jury chair and descriptions of the works, but does not provide independent analysis. Author Credibility: BBC Entertainment is a reputable source. Methodology: The article is a news report based on a press release or announcement.
